sessions.intent: Placeholder branch: capture the structural-normalization design (option 3) discovered while working on data-serialize-cleanup, so the design conversation isn't lost. Stages to be carved when work begins.

before.assumptions: data-serialize-cleanup will merge first. ISerializer takes Data, the two plang serializers have merged, signing is at the channel, Compress is flat. JSON-via-STJ-reflection is the only encoding path today.
before.risk: Design captured pre-implementation; details on lazy-vs-eager normalize, cycle detection, custom serialization extension, and the IWriter protocol shape need real coding sessions to settle. Not all questions answered.

actions.context: Initial plan and summary. Plan captures the structural normalization design: Value contract tightening, Normalize sketch, bare-when-possible wire shape, format-agnostic encoder protocol, As<T> as tree-walker, rough five-stage outline. Stages not yet carved into individual files — that happens when work starts.

after.status: Branch created as placeholder. Plan captures the design direction. Stages pending real work session.
after.health: Depends on data-serialize-cleanup landing first. No immediate action — branch sits until cleanup merges.
after.notes: Open design questions: eager vs lazy normalize (recommendation: lazy first); cycle detection mechanism; custom serialization extension surface (IDataNormalizable?); wire-attribute reuse of [Out] vs new [WireIgnore]; choice of protobuf library for proof stage.

sessions.intent: Write failing-test contract for data-normalize: [Out] discipline, Normalize+IWriter+JsonWriter, As<T> tree-walker.

before.assumptions: Architect plan settled in 3 stages with comprehensive coverage matrix; building on data-serialize-cleanup which has merged. WireJsonConverter is the single wire writer; [Out] becomes positive whitelist in Stage 2; [Masked] attribute is new; RawSignature deletion is mechanical.
before.risk: Coverage matrix has ~50 rows across three stages plus three integration cuts; risk is over-stubbing tests for behaviors that better fit existing test files. Will prune redundancies and group by concept (Stage 1 attribute placement, Normalize tree shapes, IWriter contract, As<T> reconstruction, integration cuts, failure matrix) rather than echoing architect rows 1:1.

actions.context: Plan covers 5 batches grouped by concept (not by stage): Stage 1 attributes, Normalize core, IWriter+JsonWriter+Debug, As<T>, integration+failure+goals.

Batch 1 — Stage 1 surface. Per-type [Out] inventory (13 types), RawSignature deletion verified via reflection over Data + string-scan over 3 caller files, new [Masked] attribute existence + setting.value tagging.

Batch 2 — Normalize core. Tree shape per input (primitives, lists homogeneous/heterogeneous, dicts, domain objects, idempotence, cache). Filter behavior ([Out]/[Sensitive]/[Masked], lowercased names). Cycle + depth + getter-throws.
Batch 3 — wire emission. IWriter interface surface + JsonWriter per-method bytes. Domain-shape output (path, Identity, setting, list, http.Response). Debug vs Out filter toggle.

Batch 4 — As<T> reverse direction. Generic property-bag reconstruction (primitives, lists, dicts, records). Per-type hook dispatch for path.Resolve. Negative paths (scheme mismatch, missing required, type mismatch).
Batch 5 — three integration cuts (JSON round-trip parity, debug-mode bypass, sign→wire→verify), the failure-matrix residue file, and 5 PLang goal files for developer-visible surfaces.
Session finalization. verdict.json = pass:true.

after.status: v1 failing-test contract written. 15 C# files + 5 .goal files. ~120 stubs total. Every body is Assert.Fail / throw "not implemented".
after.health: Ready for coder. Tests describe the contract; coder turns stubs into real assertions as Stages 1-3 land.
after.notes: Grouped by concept rather than stage; pruned redundant matrix rows; one goal per .goal file per memory rule.

sessions.intent: Code-review pass over data-normalize: Normalize, Reconstruct, Tagged filter, IWriter/JsonWriter, Wire converter changes, Stage 1 [Out]/[Masked]/[Store] migration. Cross-cutting scans: System.IO, Console, OBP, bare-catch.

before.assumptions: Stage 2b wiring deferred. C# 3379/3379 + PLang 233/233 + zero PLNG002 on clean rebuild. Reviewing latent defects ahead of wire-switchover.
before.risk: Stage 2b wiring will land soon; majors M1/M2 widen the wire-leak surface as soon as the value-slot pipeline switches to Normalize+JsonWriter.

actions.context: 3 majors + 8 polish. M1: json.Writer.Value default branch falls back to STJ — bypasses [Out]/[Sensitive]/[Masked] at the last gate. M2: Wire.Write emits Properties via STJ — asymmetric with the Read side that restricts to primitives. M3: EnsureInnerSigned skips IDictionary values — inner Datas held in dict values ship unsigned. P-series: Reconstruct bare-catch conflates ctor-missing with ctor-threw; Tagged inherit:true is dead-cargo; IWriter has no Enum/Float/DateTimeOffset/TimeSpan/Guid slots though Normalize keeps them as leaves; [Out,Store] on Data envelope props is dead-code; Reconstruct re-Normalize on every call; Dict<K,V> silent drop; tree-leaf list inconsistency; path 'scheme' wire field unused on inbound.

after.status: FAIL — 3 majors must be fixed before Stage 2b wiring. Report at .bot/data-normalize/codeanalyzer/v1.md.
after.health: Branch builds + tests green. Findings are latent — none failing today because Stage 2b wiring hasn't switched on the value-slot pipeline. Must address M1-M3 before that switch.
after.notes: Verified clean rebuild (zero PLNG002), 3379/3379 C# tests, 233/233 PLang tests. Next: coder addresses M1-M3, then codeanalyzer v2.

sessions.intent: Verify coder v2's fixes against codeanalyzer v1 findings. Confirm M1-M3 closed correctly, P1-P8 absorbed, no regressions.

before.assumptions: Coder v2 commit f1f83d4b9 claims all 11 v1 findings addressed. Tests claimed C# 3380/3380 + PLang 233/233.
before.risk: Verifying that the fixes are correct rather than just present.

actions.context: PASS. M1 (typed throw at gate), M2 (Normalize+Value pipeline for Properties), M3 (dict branch + fixture) all closed. P1-P8 all addressed correctly. One residual polish (V1): json.Writer.EndRecord hard-codes View.Out when normalizing inner-Data Properties — asymmetric with Wire.Write's View propagation. Theoretical harm only today since Properties holds primitives, polish-only.

after.status: FAIL — M2's fix is half-landed. Outer Wire.Write correctly propagates View into the Properties pipeline, but json.Writer.EndRecord hard-codes View.Out for inner-Data Properties. Same M2 shape at the second emission site. Report at .bot/data-normalize/codeanalyzer/v2.md.
after.health: Clean rebuild (zero PLNG002), C# 3380/3380, PLang 233/233. Tests didn't catch V1 because no fixture exercises Store-mode + inner-Data-with-Properties.
after.notes: Next: coder fixes V1 (plumb View into json.Writer ctor from Wire.Write site) + adds Store + inner-Data + Properties fixture.

sessions.intent: Verify coder v3's fix for V1 (json.Writer.EndRecord hard-coded View.Out, M2 second site).

before.assumptions: Coder v3 commit 5630ab0ad claims V1 closed by threading View into json.Writer ctor, plus a fixture covering the Store-mode + inner-Data-with-Properties path.

actions.context: PASS. View threaded through json.Writer's ctor (default Out), Wire.Write passes its View at construction. New fixture (StoreView_PropagatesIntoInnerDataProperties_NotHardcodedToOut) pins both Out-excludes and Store-includes [Sensitive] PrivateKey reachable through inner Data's Properties — covers exactly the path v2 said was uncovered.

after.status: PASS — V1 closed correctly with fixture. All v1 + v2 findings now closed. Report at .bot/data-normalize/codeanalyzer/v3.md.
after.health: Clean rebuild (zero PLNG002), C# 3381/3381 (+1 V1 fixture), PLang 233/233.

sessions.intent: Quality-validate test coverage for data-normalize v3: V1 fixture (View threading into json.Writer.EndRecord) and the broader Normalize/Reconstruct/Wire surface.

before.assumptions: codeanalyzer v3 PASSED; coder v3 closed V1 with a new fixture (StoreView_PropagatesIntoInnerDataProperties_NotHardcodedToOut); C# 3381/3381, PLang 233/233 per coder. No prior tester output exists on this branch.
before.risk: No baseline-tests.md from coder — process violation to flag. V1 fixture is a single-shot mutation test; need to verify it actually fails on mutation and that adjacent paths (collection-inside-Properties shallow gate; symmetric Store/Out asymmetry across non-json writers) are not silently uncovered.

actions.context: tester v3 outputs. Verdict FAIL: 1 PLang test red (BuilderSanity smoke — math.add on string '[1,2,3]'), coder v3 claimed 233/233 but actual is 232/233; V1 fixture honest and mutation-verified.

Mutation test: temporarily replaced _view with View.Out in EndRecord NormalizeValue call. New fixture StoreView_PropagatesIntoInnerDataProperties_NotHardcodedToOut failed as expected (PRIV-must-persist missing from store bytes). Reverted; git status clean for source.

after.health: C# green (3381/3381). PLang red (232/233) — /BuilderSanity/BuilderSanity.test.goal fails because the fixture's `set %items% = '[1, 2, 3]'` + `foreach %items%` relies on string→list auto-parse but strings are atomic under current IsPlangIterable. V1 fixture is mutation-verified honest.
after.notes: Coder v1/v2/v3 all missing baseline-tests.md — process violation flagged. 2 minor coverage notes on Wire outer-Properties path and json.Writer default-arg, both optional.

sessions.intent: Verify coder v4 closes tester v3's BuilderSanity finding (which traced to my own pre-test builder rebuild, not the branch) and that the list-literal fixture form is stable across builder reruns.

before.assumptions: Coder v4 changed Tests/BuilderSanity/BuilderSanity.test.goal:7 to use unquoted list literal [1, 2, 3] and regenerated the .pr; wrote baseline-tests.md. Claims C# 3381/3381, PLang 233/233.
before.risk: The non-determinism only fully resolves if multiple builder reruns produce stable output. If a rerun still flips %items% type, the fix is partial.

actions.context: tester v4 outputs. PASS verdict. Acknowledges v3 misattribution was procedural (pre-test rebuild on graded fixture); v4 confirms branch is honest.
Rebuilt 3x with cache=false; md5 identical (0d66eb02b8f30b03461a128a69a96218) and test passed each time. Coder v4's unquoted list-literal rewrite is deterministic.
Re-mutation test on V1 fixture: replaced _view with View.Out → fixture failed as expected. Reverted.

after.health: All green. C# 3381/3381, PLang 233/233. BuilderSanity rebuild deterministic. V1 fixture mutation-verified. baseline-tests.md present.
after.notes: v3 verdict retracted — misattribution from running cache=false rebuild on a graded fixture. Lesson saved to tester memory.

sessions.intent: Blue+red security audit of data-normalize: Normalize/Reconstruct/Wire/json.Writer/Tagged + attribute migrations.

before.assumptions: codeanalyzer v3 PASS (V1 closed, M-series clean); tester v4 PASS (3381/3381 + 233/233); Wire.Read depth cap from data-serialize-cleanup is carried forward through the rename; AuthGate canonicalisation from purge-systemio-from-actions/v2 closes the lexical-prefix bypass that Reconstruct's path hook depends on.
before.risk: Two new recursive surfaces (Normalize, Reconstruct.Walk) plus a reflection-driven Tagged filter. Untagged-type transparent fallback in Tagged is a latent footgun for any future domain type that lands in a Data value graph and forgets [Sensitive].

actions.context: v1 plan / result / verdict / summary / branch-level security-report. PASS verdict.

Normalize: MaxNormalizeDepth=128, ReferenceEqualityComparer cycle set, leaf table consistent with IsTreeLeafType, Masked never invokes getter, getter-throws wrapped. Reconstruct: Walk relies on Wire.Read's MaxReadDepth=64 for wire trees; T is caller-controlled (no type-confusion). Wire: depth cap carried through rename; Properties primitives-only via ReadPropertyPrimitive; hash-outer ref-counted; sign-if-missing requires Context.Actor. Transport: gzip-decompress already capped at 100MB.

json.Writer: _view threaded via ctor (codeanalyzer v3 V1 closed), default case throws NormalizeException — fails closed on unknown leaf type, prevents STJ reflection bypass of Out/Sensitive/Masked. Tagged: per-(type,mode) cache; transparent fallback for types with no four-tags = ships all public — design-aware footgun (memory note). plang serializer: three options bags (Out / In / Store), no view drift at call sites.

Attribute migrations: Identity PrivateKey stays [Sensitive] + gains [Store]; setting.value [Out, Masked, Store]; Variable.Name [Out] only (RawValue excluded — correct, secrets can live in raw refs); path.Scheme + path.Relative [Out, Store] (Absolute excluded — sandbox-correct); http.Response Status/Headers/Body [Out]. [Masked] new attribute.

Sqlite.Set switched to Store view (correct — Identity.PrivateKey round-trips); Ed25519.VerifyAsync now reads .Signature directly (RawSignature deleted); permission flow swaps RawSignature → Signature (semantic-equivalent). Signature mutation sites enumerated — Wire.Read, WrapAsTyped, Normalize copy, Ed25519 sign — all proper code paths.

Semgrep baseline scan — 15 findings, matches baseline. No new files added to the plang-serializer-default-options list; builder/code/Default.cs:321 plan-dump matches the existing 14 sites (planValue is LLM-JSON, no path/Sensitive reachability).

after.health: Clean — no critical/high findings. Verdict: pass. Branch ready for auditor.
after.notes: Two design-aware notes recorded for memory: (1) Tagged transparent fallback ships all public props for untagged types — architect-intentional but a latent footgun the next domain-type author must remember; (2) BuilderPlannerFailed plan dump uses default JsonSerializer.Serialize — matches existing semgrep baseline. Next bot: auditor.
